focus: [D5-A5/D5] smart-router blindness - add a DESIGN intent to each cli-* router or an ALWAYS baseline so routine design prompts load the design contract

graphEvents.label: Do cli-* smart routers recognize routine design prompts strongly enough to load the design dispatch contract?
cli-opencode, cli-codex, and cli-claude-code have no DESIGN intent lane in their smart-router dictionaries
cli-* ALWAYS rules pass spec folders and code standards but do not carry a design standards loading rule
cli-opencode has a design/UI task template, but it is not a cross-CLI router guarantee
Every cli-* dispatch surface must either route design prompts to a DESIGN contract resource or apply an ALWAYS design manifest baseline

label: cli-opencode, cli-codex, and cli-claude-code have no DESIGN intent lane in their smart-router dictionaries
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for static router lint and replay fixtures; ADVISORY for novel runtime phrasing beyond the corpus
recommendation: Add DESIGN to every cli-* INTENT_SIGNALS map and route it to a skill-local references/design_dispatch_contract.md because current router guards constrain resources to the current skill root.

label: cli-* ALWAYS rules pass spec folders and code standards but do not carry a design standards loading rule
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for required ALWAYS text and negative prompt replay; ADVISORY for borderline design-adjacent classification
recommendation: Add a Design Standards Loading ALWAYS rule after Code Standards Loading in all three CLI skills, requiring DESIGN_DISPATCH_MANIFEST v1 for UI/design/visual/accessibility/motion/Figma/Open Design delegation.

label: cli-opencode has a design/UI task template, but it is not a cross-CLI router guarantee
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for template parity and router reachability; ADVISORY for visual quality after the manifest is satisfied
recommendation: Promote the compact manifest from cli-opencode Template 16 into a shared generated design_dispatch_contract.md copied into each CLI skill references folder; keep Template 16 as the expanded OpenCode scaffold.

label: Every cli-* dispatch surface must either route design prompts to a DESIGN contract resource or apply an ALWAYS design manifest baseline
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE by static token lint plus smart-router replay on positive and negative fixtures.

direction: Rely on existing cli-opencode Template 16 alone
reason: It is local to cli-opencode assets and is not reachable as a design-specific router intent across cli-codex and cli-claude-code.
